title: Assisted Living Search Checklist
summary: For touring, comparing, and choosing a community.

tasks.title: Clarify budget and funding sources
tasks.explanation: Private funds, long-term care insurance, veterans benefits.
tasks.notes: Use the Care Cost Estimator first.

tasks.title: List must-haves vs. nice-to-haves
tasks.explanation: Specific care needs, location, room type, memory care unit if needed.

tasks.title: Schedule 2–3 tours, including one during a meal
tasks.explanation: Daily life is more visible at meals than in a lobby tour.

tasks.title: Ask about staff-to-resident ratio and turnover
tasks.explanation: More predictive of care quality than amenities.

tasks.title: Ask how care plans are reassessed as needs change
tasks.explanation: And what triggers a move to a higher (costlier) care tier.

tasks.title: Talk to a current resident or family member if possible
tasks.explanation: Often more revealing than the official tour.

tasks.title: Get an itemized price list, not just a general quote
tasks.explanation: Communities must provide one on request.

tasks.title: Read the full contract, including rate-increase and refund policies
tasks.explanation: Have a financial advisor or elder law attorney review it if possible.
tasks.jurisdictionNote: Contract terms and state oversight of assisted living vary significantly.

tasks.title: Check state inspection reports for the community
tasks.explanation: Usually available through the state licensing agency.
